source_files: experimental/holograph-runtime/adapters/process_adapter.py — emits {pid, name, cpu_pct, mem_mb} per /proc entry every 5s
experimental/holograph-runtime/src/handlers.js — process-observer was the original stub facet
notes: Tile mesh: small green-emissive box (0x000900 = 2304). One tile per server-process Thing. cpu_color_low/med/high (green/orange/red) reserved for the future cpu-color-driven render that paints tile material by cpu_pct.

stale_seconds_until_despawn = 30 — matches the process-observer stub's 30s stale-cutoff. The real wewon/quandaleServer adapter will publish lastSeen every ~5s; if lastSeen > now-30s, despawn (process died).
label rendering deferred — needs a future label-sprite facet that places a CanvasTexture sprite above the tile with the process name.

For substrate today: demo spawns are fixed pid/name placeholders. The real /proc feed lands when the WebSocket bridge from quandaleServer publishes process-adapter events into the in-browser registry.
